PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker, via ZOOM, counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator, via ZOOM, Administrator.

At the outset, the court queries counsel as to why they filed a Status Petition instead of an Accounting Petition. Counsel reports that they believed that by filing the Status Report in lieu of an Accounting, that they would save resources as there is only about $23,000 remaining in the Estate and, that it would be better use of judicial resources. Additionally, the Status Report includes notice to heirs. The court then cites that under Probate code 10950, the court may order an accounting at any time, on its own motion.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-22-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.
Brian Connors for Robert Spitler (Administrator of the Estate of Richard Dixon).

The Court noted that the objection goes as to the asset of the estate, not as to the issue of appointment. Mr. Connors stated that the Court is correct, that his objection goes as to the asset of the estate, that the Public Administrator filed a declaration indicating that the 806 Potrero property is 100% owned by the Elinor estate, and that it implicates whether his Client took proper control of that property and takes away his right to sell that property. Ms. Mannion stated that Mr. Connors' Client doesn't have standing to object, the he is not an heir, devisee, child, spouse or creditor, that the Richard estate does not have a pecuniary interest in the Elinor estate and that it is an estimate of the property owned by the Decedent. Ms. Mannion also stated that they are here to be appointed on an estate that they were essentially already administering. The Court stated that it makes no finding of any property in or outside of the estate. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Status on close of estate is set for March 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Continued To Oct-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: Brian Connors for Robert Spitler (Personal Representative in the Estate of Richard Dixon; PES-18-302101).

Matter called. Mr. Connors stated that this was an unusual situation, that the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA) designated the property at 806 Potrero as an asset of the Elinor Dixon estate when it was an asset of the Richard Dixon estate, that the property was sold, and that the SFPA was given full notice of the report of sale. The Court stated that it would continue the matter to notify the SFPA and that notice should be given to Janet Boessenecker. Mr. Connors requested a continuance to November 13, 2019 stating that the SFPA is in litigation in the Estate of Richard Dixon; PES-18-302101 and that the Estate of Richard Dixon is on calendar that date. The Court continued the matter to October 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Connors inquired about notice to the SFPA. Ms. Hilton stated that Probate Examiner Sean Bang would provide notice of the continuance to SFPA.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.
